name: verify-postgresql-firestore-replacement
description: Verify that all PostgreSQL/Cloud SQL references in documentation were correctly replaced with Firestore. Check each of 14 files. Fix any missed or incorrect replacements.
instruction:
1. Grep for remaining PostgreSQL/Postgres/Cloud SQL references in track_changes/.documentation/
2. For each remaining hit: read context, replace with Firestore
3. Check ARC-FINAL-ITRP-application.md most carefully — it's the architecture doc
4. Verify the architecture diagram text shows Firestore, not Cloud SQL
5. Check imp_plan.md tasks — any that reference PostgreSQL migrations, SQL schemas, Cloud SQL setup must be updated
6. Check DECISIONS.md — ADR entries about Cloud SQL should be updated to reflect Firestore decision
7. Check NFR-001 — performance requirements referencing PostgreSQL response times
8. Verify no broken references (e.g. 'Firestore row-level lock' which doesn't exist)
acceptance_criteria: Zero grep hits for PostgreSQL, Postgres, Cloud SQL in .documentation/
No references to SQL-specific features (row-level locks, SQL migrations) without Firestore equivalent noted

name: create-discrepancies-document
description: Create DISCREPANCIES.md documenting all contradictions, unclear items, and things that need client clarification. Sources: documentation vs code, documentation vs documentation, open items from analysis files.
instruction:
Create track_changes/.documentation/DISCREPANCIES.md with sections:

1. ARCHITECTURE DISCREPANCIES
- Original ARC-FINAL said PostgreSQL, code uses Firestore (RESOLVED: Firestore stays)
- Original ARC-FINAL said direct BigQuery cross-project reads from Warsaw, actual: own temp_invoice table (RESOLVED)
- Original ARC-FINAL said PG->BQ nightly sync, actual: dual-write at purchase time (RESOLVED)
- ARC-FINAL mentions Redis elimination but code never had Redis
- ARC-FINAL mentions Next.js SSR but current code uses client-side rendering with useSearchParams

2. BUSINESS RULES UNCLEAR
- Credit limit: source (Warsaw or manual?), threshold enforcement (pause vs reject?)
- Mid Office role: exact permissions undefined ('limited' not specified)
- Week boundary: UTC or country-local time? (TODO.md item 1.1)
- File completion gate: mechanism undefined (TODO.md item S.1)
- Step 0 data quality: validation rules TBD
- sales_type field vs sell_invoice boolean (TODO.md item 2.1)
- intercompany field confirmation needed (TODO.md item 2.2)
- Priority expiry after run: clear or persist? (TODO.md item 3.3)

3. DOCUMENTATION vs CODE GAPS
- Dashboard: doc describes status overview per country, code has 7-step orchestration runner
- ITRP main: doc describes full financial picture with cashflow, code has simple preview+execute
- Settings: doc describes start dates + auto-load + auto-buy schedules, code has only auto-buy schedule
- Maintenance: doc describes Load Data + Backup Restore, code has placeholder tabs
- Reporting: doc describes 4 report types, code has stub endpoint
- My Customers: doc describes customer management, code has nothing

4. OPEN ITEMS FROM ANALYSIS FILES
- Read each analysis file (AUTO-BUY, CREDIT-LIMIT, SCHEDULER, etc) and extract unresolved items

5. DEPENDENCIES ON EXTERNAL TEAMS
- Warsaw BigQuery schema contract (DEP-005)
- Apigee setup
- GCP access (reported 50%)
- Business rules finalization (20% incomplete, deadline passed)
- Mockup approval status unknown
acceptance_criteria: Each discrepancy has status: RESOLVED, OPEN, or NEEDS_CLARIFICATION
Open items from all analysis files included

name: reorganize-documentation-structure
description: Reorganize .documentation/ into clear categories. Create an INDEX.md that maps every document to its purpose and status.
instruction:
Create track_changes/.documentation/INDEX.md with:

## Documentation Index

### Architecture & Design
- ARC-FINAL-ITRP-application.md — Final architecture (Firestore + BigQuery, modular monolith)
- VIS-001-ITRP-application.md — Visual design specifications
- DECISIONS.md — Architecture Decision Records

### Requirements
- REQ-001-ITRP-application.md — Functional requirements
- NFR-001-ITRP-application.md — Non-functional requirements
- DEP-001-ITRP-application.md — Dependencies and external teams

### Implementation
- project.md — Full technical specification (MASTER REFERENCE)
- imp_plan.md — Implementation plan with phases and tasks
- ITRP_Knowledge_Base.md — Domain knowledge base

### Page Specifications (UI)
- pages/01-dashboard.md through pages/08-user-flow.md

### Analysis Documents
- AUTO-BUY-analysis.md, CREDIT-LIMIT-CHECK-analysis.md, etc.
- SPEC-auto-buy-schedule-maintenance.md

### Project Management
- Meeting-notes/ — Meeting records
- DISCREPANCIES.md — Open issues and contradictions

For each document: one line with title, what it covers, and whether it's CURRENT/OUTDATED/NEEDS-UPDATE.

Also review project.md (the master spec) for any remaining PostgreSQL references and fix them.
acceptance_criteria: INDEX.md exists with categorized list of all documents
Each document has status: CURRENT, NEEDS-UPDATE, or OUTDATED

name: align-imp-plan-with-reality
description: Update imp_plan.md to reflect actual code state. Mark completed tasks, adjust task descriptions to match Firestore reality, add missing tasks discovered from code analysis.
instruction:
Read imp_plan.md fully. For each TASK-XXX:
1. Check if the task's deliverable already exists in code (use code analysis from K-001)
2. If DONE in code: mark as DONE with note what implements it
3. If PARTIAL: note what's done and what remains
4. If task references PostgreSQL: update to Firestore equivalent
5. If task is blocked by external dependency: note the blocker

Specific updates needed:
- TASK-001 (GCP Setup): PARTIAL — Firestore and BQ exist, Cloud Run TBD
- TASK-003 (Auth): DONE — Mock SSO implemented
- TASK-005 (Firestore Setup): DONE — seed data creates 13 collections
- TASK-301 (RBAC): DONE — full CRUD with transactions
- TASK-302 (Audit): DONE — immutable logging
- TASK-401 (Buying Service): PARTIAL — steps 1-7 exist but with gaps (see TODO.md)
- Tasks referencing 'PostgreSQL schema' or 'SQL migrations': rewrite for Firestore

Also add any tasks that are in the code but NOT in the plan (e.g. scheduler trigger endpoint).
acceptance_criteria: Every TASK in imp_plan.md has status: DONE, PARTIAL, TODO, or BLOCKED
